title: Disagreement Taxonomy and Decision Semantics Analysis
partial_definition_mission_brain: Mission Brain classifies a goal as 'partial' when: the supervised run terminated without full success (blocked/failed), but evidence exists that partial goal criteria were met — e.g. some sub-tasks executed, some tests passed, or the goal was partially addressed. This is a GOAL-LEVEL assessment: how much of the stated goal was achieved across all attempts, regardless of whether the last run succeeded.
failed_definition_current_loop: The current supervisor loop classifies an outcome as 'failed' when the supervised run attempt does not complete successfully: tests fail, smoke checks fail, or the run is blocked (workspace dirty, infrastructure bug, pytest failure, etc.). This is a RUN-LEVEL assessment: did THIS ATTEMPT succeed or not? It is a binary outcome: success | failure. Partial progress within the run is not surfaced — only the terminal outcome matters.
divergence_explanation: The divergence is structural and not a sign of MB malfunction. Mission Brain evaluates at the GOAL level (how much of the goal was achieved?) while the loop evaluates at the RUN level (did this attempt succeed?). Since all 30 cycles had blocked/failed runs, the loop always returns 'failed' (run-level binary). MB returns 'partial' because it detects goal-level partial progress despite the block. These two assessments are not contradictory — they measure different properties. For 3 goal classes (ambiguous_goal, empty_context, conflicting_signals), the divergence has an additional component: goal ambiguity/insufficient context makes MB's 'partial' non-informative (not real partial progress, just uncertainty).

per_cycle_classification.notes: Some policy criteria can be verified even on a blocked run; partial is informative.
Partial risk evaluation has value even if run is blocked.
Loop coherence checks may partially pass; partial reflects this.
Planning steps are sequential; partial completion on a blocked run is real.
Some tests may run before block; partial coverage is factual.
Boundary criteria partially evaluated before block.
Decomposition sub-goals partially resolved before block.
Some git safety checks may pass; partial is factual for pre-block checks.
Verification has sub-criteria; partial captures those met before block.
Memory analysis can proceed partially on a blocked run.
Some policy criteria can be verified even on a blocked run; partial is informative.
Partial risk evaluation has value even if run is blocked.
Verification has sub-criteria; partial captures those met before block.
Goal is ambiguous by design; partial reflects uncertainty, not real progress. Contested.
No context to evaluate against; partial is not informative. Contested.
Conflicting criteria: partial may mask real failure. Contested.
Multi-step goals: earlier steps may succeed; partial is accurate.
Even simple verification has sub-steps; partial possible if loop blocked early.
Some regression checks may run before block; partial is factual.
Dependency checks can be partially resolved; partial is factual.

calibration_rationale: Replace the single mismatch class 'safe_more_optimistic_mission_brain' with two more precise classes: (1) 'scope_mismatch_goal_vs_run_assessment' for cases where MB and loop measure different scopes (17 cycles), and (2) 'expected_divergence_ambiguous_context' for cases where goal ambiguity drives the divergence (3 cycles). This change does NOT alter agreement_rate (still 0.0) but provides accurate understanding of WHY they diverge, which is necessary before any integration.
